value.description: Claim value. The runtime may return any JSON value.

emergency_change_class.description: Must appear in the signed target change_classes and be authorized by local trust roots.

reason.description: Local approval reason. Audit records store only a hash of this value.

ClaimProvenance.description: Versioned claim provenance (registry-notary-claim-provenance/v1). PROV-mappable but not PROV-O. Requester-side identity (client, actor, subject) is deliberately absent; it lives in restricted audit only.

derived_from.description: Upstream provenance records this result was derived from. Always empty in v1; reserved for additive cross-evaluation linking.

ClaimRef.description: Claim reference. Wire requests may also use a plain string claim id.

value.description: Claim value. The runtime may return any JSON value.

ClaimSemantics.description: Optional semantic binding for a Notary claim. These fields label the claim output or predicate with external vocabulary terms such as PublicSchema URIs; they do not change the Notary result shape or by themselves prove privacy minimization.

concept.description: External concept URI, for example https://publicschema.org/Person.

derived_from.description: External property URIs the predicate or derived value depends on.

predicate.description: External or local predicate URI/URN for derived boolean claims.

property.description: External property URI for raw value claims, for example https://publicschema.org/date_of_birth.

value_mapping.description: Operator label for the value mapping/canonicalization applied before returning the claim.

vocabulary.description: External vocabulary or value-set URI used by the claim value.

target_inputs.description: Safe request-input metadata for building evaluation targets. It exposes target-side paths and matching method labels, never connector, connection, dataset, entity, or source-field details.

path.description: Target-side request path, for example target.identifiers.national_id or target.attributes.birthdate.

confidence.description: Policy-asserted confidence label for this target-input method.

groups.description: OR-of-AND input groups. A request needs to satisfy one group.

policy_id.description: Stable target-matching policy identifier when configured.

break_glass.description: Apply-only emergency mode. Verify and dry-run reject break-glass requests.

break_glass_approval_reference.description: Apply-only reference for a matching durable break-glass approval record in the verifier-owned local approval store.

bundle_id.description: Operator-visible candidate identifier. Signed TUF targets may derive it from target metadata.

config_yaml.description: Inline YAML candidate for verify and dry-run diagnostics.

local_approval_reference.description: Apply-only reference for a matching local approval record used by root_transition bundles.

previous_config_hash.description: Governed predecessor config hash. Requests and signed target metadata may provide either bare lowercase SHA-256 hex or sha256:<64 lowercase hex>; responses, audit, docs, and errors use the canonical sha256:-prefixed form.

sequence.description: Monotonic bundle sequence. Signed TUF targets may derive it from target metadata.

stream_id.description: Governance stream identifier used by anti-rollback state.

detail.description: Optional diagnostic detail. Previous-config-hash mismatch details use canonical sha256:<64 lowercase hex> for the expected value and name the detected received format.

posture_result.description: Posture projection of the outcome, such as accepted, rejected, or not_applied.

result.description: Config apply outcome, such as applied, verified, rejected_restart_required, rejected_readiness, or rejected_rollback.

assurance.description: Optional assurance level of the actor (for example an acr value).

id_hash.description: Keyed-hash identifier of the actor in hmac-sha256:<hex> format. Never a raw principal value.

EvidenceOnBehalfOf.description: Frozen minimal actor/delegation envelope. Replaces the previous free-form object; free-form payloads are rejected. Simple deployments omit on_behalf_of entirely. Production-grade delegation (OAuth token exchange / RAR / CIBA) arrives post-1.0 as an additive profile that maps the actor onto OAuth act-claim semantics via the opaque delegation_ref; the shape does not bake in a single-actor assumption.

delegation_ref.description: Opaque reference to an out-of-band delegation record. Not interpreted by the envelope; the indirection point through which a later OAuth profile resolves an actor chain.

confidence.description: Policy-asserted confidence label configured on the source binding and method. This is returned verbatim for successful matches against that binding; it is not a measured quality score for the individual match.

method.description: Configured matching method for the source binding.

policy_id.description: Target-matching policy identifier for the source binding: the policy that governs how the request target is matched to a source record. Distinct from generated_by.policy_id in claim provenance, which names the evaluation policy under which the result was produced.

score.description: Reserved optional per-match score. Current configured-only matching usually omits this field.

policy_hash.description: sha256:<hex> digest of the evaluation policy. Public in v1: a hash revealing no policy content, used to correlate the result with a policy evidence-pack.

policy_id.description: Evaluation policy identifier: the policy under which this result was produced. Distinct from matching.policy_id, which names the target-matching policy for a source binding. Present for flows evaluated under a named policy (e.g. self-attestation); omitted for machine-client flows with no evaluation policy.

service_id.description: Identifier of the service that produced the result. Replaces the dropped computed_by field; the CCCEV renderer maps its provider agent from here.

source_runtimes.description: Minimized summaries for connectors that cross an external execution boundary (the OpenFn sidecar). The full assurance document stays in restricted audit.

RemoteTufConfigTargetRequest.description: Remote TUF config source. The source must exactly match an entry in the operator-configured config_trust.remote_tuf_repositories allowlist (comparing root_path, metadata_base_url, targets_base_url, and datastore_dir); unlisted sources are rejected before any fetch.

allow_dev_insecure_fetch_urls.description: Deprecated and ignored. Fetch policy is taken from the matching config_trust.remote_tuf_repositories allowlist entry.

metadata_base_url.description: HTTPS TUF metadata base URL. HTTP loopback is accepted only when the matching allowlist entry sets allow_dev_insecure_fetch_urls.

targets_base_url.description: HTTPS TUF targets base URL. HTTP loopback is accepted only when the matching allowlist entry sets allow_dev_insecure_fetch_urls.

config_hash.description: sha256:<hex> digest of the runtime configuration.

tx_code.description: The numeric PIN shown on the offer page. Required when the credential offer includes a tx_code object.

info.description: Standalone claim evaluation, rendering, and credential issuance API.
info.summary: Standalone evidence evaluation, rendering, and credential issuance service.
info.title: Registry Notary API

get.description: Returns public issuer verification keys for wallet and verifier discovery. This well-known route is intentionally unauthenticated; it only exposes public JWK members.
Returns the OpenID4VCI issuer metadata for Registry Notary' dc+sd-jwt issuance profile.

display.description: Proof that the civil registry currently records this person as alive.
display.name: Person is alive

get.description: Returns public SD-JWT VC Type Metadata at the SD-JWT VC well-known location. Consumers dereference an HTTPS vct by inserting /.well-known/vct between the host and the path; the server strips that prefix and matches the reconstructed vct (https://{host}/{vct_path}) against a configured OID4VCI credential configuration. The path key uses the OpenAPI single-segment form {vct_path} for standard tooling compatibility, but the route is a multi-segment catch-all: vct_path captures the full path remainder (slashes included, not percent-encoded) and may span multiple segments. This route is intentionally unversioned and must remain at /.well-known/vct/{vct_path}: per RFC 8615 well-known URI semantics, the path prefix is determined by the protocol and cannot be changed. Adding a /v1/ prefix would violate the well-known URI convention. This route serves type metadata only and is unrelated to POST /v1/credentials (credential issuance).

parameters.description: Full path remainder after /.well-known/vct/ captured as a multi-segment catch-all. The value is the unencoded path suffix of the VCT URL (slashes not percent-encoded) and may contain one or more segments.
parameters.name: vct_path

display.description: Proof that the civil registry currently records this person as alive.
display.name: Person is alive

404.description: OpenID4VCI issuer is disabled or no configured vct matches the reconstructed URL

get.summary: Fetch SD-JWT VC Type Metadata at the well-known location

get.description: Returns redacted product capability metadata for governed configuration, posture, and reload operations.

200.description: Admin capabilities for this product runtime

post.description: Standalone mode applies only signed local TUF config targets. Governed signed credential issuer key rotations can swap the issuer runtime after anti-rollback acceptance. Break-glass apply additionally requires approval details, a locally configured rate-limit policy, and a signed emergency change class. Inline config candidates are rejected with registry.admin.config.inline_apply_rejected. Other signed changes remain restart-required.

200.description: Compatible signed config was applied without restart

example.detail: candidate config could not be parsed
example.title: Candidate config invalid

409.description: Candidate config requires restart and was not applied

post.summary: Attempt to apply a candidate runtime config

post.description: Standalone mode validates an inline candidate config or verifies a local or remote signed TUF config target. Inline candidates and non-swappable changes report rejected_restart_required without mutating runtime state.

200.description: Candidate config was evaluated without applying

example.detail: candidate config could not be parsed
example.title: Candidate config invalid

post.description: Standalone mode parses and validates an inline candidate config or verifies a local or remote signed TUF config target. Governed signed credential issuer key rotations may be hot-applied when anti-rollback state accepts the bundle.

example.detail: candidate config could not be parsed
example.title: Candidate config invalid

example.title: Invalid credential status

example.title: Credential status not found

post.description: Standalone mode does not support runtime configuration reload. Operators should call /admin/v1/capabilities before invoking product-specific reload operations.

example.detail: registry-notary standalone runtime does not support reload
example.message: registry-notary standalone runtime does not support reload
example.title: Admin capability not supported

501.description: Runtime configuration reload is not supported

get.description: Returns public SD-JWT VC Type Metadata for a configured OID4VCI credential configuration whose vct exactly matches the requested absolute URL. The path key uses the OpenAPI single-segment form {vct_path} for standard tooling compatibility, but the route is a multi-segment catch-all: vct_path captures the full path remainder after /credentials/ (slashes included, not percent-encoded) and may span multiple segments. This route is intentionally unversioned and must remain at /credentials/{vct_path} (without a /v1/ prefix): per SD-JWT VC type-metadata dereference, a client resolves the credential type by dereferencing the vct claim directly (for example, https://{host}/credentials/{vct_path}), so the server path must match the vct URL path component exactly. Adding a /v1/ prefix would break dereference for any credential whose vct does not include that prefix. This route serves type metadata only and is unrelated to POST /v1/credentials (credential issuance).

parameters.description: Full path remainder after /credentials/ captured as a multi-segment catch-all. The value is the unencoded path suffix of the VCT URL (slashes not percent-encoded) and may contain one or more segments.
parameters.name: vct_path

display.description: Proof that the civil registry currently records this person as alive.
display.name: Person is alive

404.description: OpenID4VCI issuer is disabled or no configured vct matches the requested URL

post.description: Accepts a compact JWS request with typ registry-notary-request+jwt. This route is mounted only when federation is enabled and uses body-JWT authentication instead of API key or bearer authentication.

schema.description: Compact JWS signed federation evaluation request

200.description: Compact JWS signed federation evaluation response

403.description: Peer, profile, purpose, or requester/target identity path is not allowed

503.description: Source service or peer key service is unavailable

post.summary: Evaluate one configured federation profile for a trusted peer

post.description: Issues a dc+sd-jwt credential for an authenticated self-attestation principal. Error responses use the OpenID4VCI error envelope, not RFC 9457 Problem Details.

400.description: Invalid credential request, proof, or type

get.description: Returns an authorization-code credential offer. Error responses use the OpenID4VCI error envelope, not RFC 9457 Problem Details.

post.description: Returns a c_nonce for proof-of-possession. Error responses use the OpenID4VCI error envelope, not RFC 9457 Problem Details.

get.description: Public and unauthenticated. Consumes the login state, exchanges the eSignet code with private_key_jwt, validates the id_token, and mints one single-use pre-authorized_code. When configured, the offer also includes one numeric tx_code (PIN) shown out-of-band from the QR. Returns 404 when the pre-authorized-code flow is disabled.

200.description: Offer page with the credential offer URI and optional tx_code PIN

400.description: Login state, eSignet code, or id_token is invalid

get.summary: Complete eSignet login and render a pre-authorized-code offer

get.description: Public and unauthenticated. Begins the eSignet authorization-code login as the confidential RP and 302-redirects the browser to eSignet. Mints no pre-authorized_code or credential material. Returns 404 when the pre-authorized-code flow is disabled. Error responses use the OpenID4VCI error envelope, not RFC 9457 Problem Details.

303.description: Redirect to the eSignet authorization endpoint

post.description: Public and unauthenticated OID4VCI token endpoint for the pre-authorized-code grant. Accepts only grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:pre-authorized_code with a valid, unexpired, single-use pre-authorized_code. A matching tx_code is required when the credential offer includes a tx_code object. Mints a short-TTL Notary-signed access token plus a c_nonce. Returns 404 when the pre-authorized-code flow is disabled. Error responses use the OpenID4VCI error envelope, not RFC 9457 Problem Details.

example.error_description: pre-authorized code or tx_code is invalid

429.description: Too many token attempts (wrong-PIN lockout or random-code flood)

post.summary: Redeem a pre-authorized-code for an access token
